etymology.description: Depicts an infant with an imperfect cranium -> represents 'child'
etymology.interpretation: The pictogram captures the distinctive shape of a child's head, with the undeveloped cranium represented as open. This directly conveys the idea of a young child or infant.

examples.chinese: 那个小孩(那個小孩)在公园里玩耍(在公園裡玩耍)。
examples.pinyin: Nàge xiǎohái zài gōngyuán lǐ wánshuǎ.
examples.english: The child is playing in the park.

examples.chinese: 她的儿子(她的兒子)聪明又好学(聰明又好學)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de érzi cōngmíng yòu hàoxué.
examples.english: Her child is smart and eager to learn.

examples.chinese: 每个家庭(每個家庭)都希望他们的孩子(都希望他們的孩子)健康快乐(健康快樂)。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè jiātíng dōu xīwàng tāmen de háizi jiànkāng kuàilè.
examples.english: Every family hopes their children are healthy and happy.
